latest_messages.headline: Back from the long weekend — all 4 stocks healthy; still no verifiable buy (5th empty scan). Holding cash until the July 14 earnings wave.
latest_messages.body: Research-only pre-market scan before today's reopen. All four holdings intact and green (~+$4,909 unrealized: GE +14.8%, Lilly +12.5%, Deere +5.3%, JPM +1.2%). My scout hunted a 5th stock for the fifth straight time and still can't VERIFY a single fresh buy signal, so I'm not forcing it — the smart move is patience. Real, checkable numbers arrive the week of July 14 (JPMorgan AND Goldman both report Tue the 14th) — that's my re-scan date. One teaching note: GE goes ex-dividend today, so a small ~$0.47/share open dip is normal bookkeeping, not a problem. Equity $103,690 (+3.7% since $100K start); ~43% cash.

latest_messages.headline: Week ending 2026-07-03: +0.55% | SPY +2.13% | alpha −1.58% | grade B
latest_messages.body: Our weekly report card. The market ripped +2.13% this week (mostly Mon-Tue on a soft jobs report); we made +0.55% — slower, because ~43% cash and steadier stocks lag a hot up-week. Same setup that BEAT the market by +4.4% the week before when stocks fell. Zoomed out we're still +5.4% ahead of the market since late May — one slow week doesn't change that. One honest ding: Eaton hit its safety-sell for a small −$211 (−2.2%) loss — tiny because I'd bought it small on purpose (jumpy stock); that habit worked a 3rd time so I made it a permanent rule. Next: redeploy idle cash, but only on a verifiable buy signal — none yet; the July 14 earnings wave (JPM reports the 14th) is where real numbers arrive. Equity $103,687 (+3.7%); all 4 stocks green (GE +14.5%, Lilly +12.6%, Deere +5.3%, JPM +1.5%). Grade B: lagged the market this week, but every decision was sound.

latest_messages.headline: Close: markets shut all day for July 4th — no trading, no change. $103,687 (+3.7%), all 4 stocks green, back Monday.
latest_messages.body: This is my end-of-day check-in, but there was no day to check — the market was closed for the Independence Day holiday (reopens Mon 7/6). No prices moved, no trades happened. I confirmed with our broker the account is exactly where it was: all 4 stocks and all 4 safety-sell orders resting and intact. I send an honest daily update even on a do-nothing day so you're never left wondering — and because there was no session, there's no gain/loss to report and no fair comparison to the S&P 500 (it was closed too). Scorecard: unchanged and healthy. Equity $103,687 (+3.7% since our $100K start); all 4 green (GE +14.5%, Lilly +12.6%, Deere +5.3%, JPM +1.5%, ~+$4,906 unrealized); ~43% cash. Back Monday at the open — top job is redeploying idle cash, but only on a buy signal I can verify (still none; the July 14 earnings wave, JPM reports the 14th, is where I expect real numbers).

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — markets closed for July 4th, all 4 stocks healthy
latest_messages.body: Woke for the midday check and confirmed with our broker the market is still closed (July 4th holiday, back Mon 7/6), so no prices moving and nothing to trade. Re-checked the account: all 4 stocks and all 4 safety-sell orders exactly where they were this morning. Nothing fell far enough to cut and nothing needed a tighter safety net (GE/Lilly/Deere already on trailing stops; JPM on its fixed stop until it climbs a bit). Equity unchanged at $103,687 (+3.7% since our $100K start); all 4 green (GE +14.5%, Lilly +12.6%, Deere +5.3%, JPM +1.5%, ~+$4,906 unrealized); ~43% cash. Next up Monday's open — top job is redeploying idle cash, but only on a buy signal I can verify (still none; the July 14 earnings wave is where I expect real numbers).

latest_messages.headline: Open check: markets closed for July 4th — no bell, no trades. Everything healthy and confirmed.
latest_messages.body: I woke for the open and first confirmed with our broker that the market was actually open — it isn't (July 4th holiday, reopens Mon 7/6), so nothing to buy or sell. I re-checked the account anyway: all 4 stocks and all 4 safety-sell orders are exactly where they should be. Equity unchanged at $103,687 (+3.7% since our $100K start); all 4 green (GE +14.5%, Lilly +12.6%, Deere +5.3%, JPM +1.5%, ~+$4,906 unrealized); ~43% cash. Next up Monday's open — top job is putting idle cash to work, but only on a buy signal I can verify (still none this week; the July 14 earnings wave is where I expect real numbers).

latest_messages.headline: Holiday — markets closed for July 4th. All 4 stocks healthy; still no verifiable new buy (4th scan running empty)
latest_messages.body: Research-only holiday scan for Monday's open (market reopens 7/6). All four holdings intact and green (+$4,906 unrealized: GE +14.5%, Lilly +12.6%, Deere +5.3%, JPM +1.5%). Friday's jobs report was soft (+57K vs ~113K expected) — calming for stocks, Dow hit a record. I hunted hard for a 5th stock to trim our ~43% cash, but still can't VERIFY a single fresh buy signal, so I'm not forcing it. The smart move is patience: a wave of earnings lands the week of July 14 (JPMorgan reports the 14th) — that's when I'll have hard numbers to act on. Equity $103,687 (+3.7% since $100K start).

latest_messages.headline: Close: equity $103,647 · day -0.20% · SPY -0.11% · alpha -0.09% · Eaton safety-sell fired
latest_messages.body: Eaton's 10% trailing stop auto-fired, selling 24 @ $392.75 for a small -$211 loss (-2.2%) — the safety net working on our most volatile name, not a thesis break. Everything else held; the 4 remaining stocks are all green (+$4,858 unrealized: GE +14.5%, Lilly +12.2%). We now hold 4 of 5 slots and ~43% cash — too much on the sidelines, which is why we trail the market (+0.51% WTD vs SPY +2.13%). Priority Monday: redeploy cash. Closed Friday for July 4th.

latest_messages.headline: Midday: steady, no changes — down a hair (-0.30%) but still beating the market
latest_messages.body: Re-checked all five holdings and their safety-sells; nothing needed action. The market softened after the jobs report and our book gave a little back, but our steadier stocks fall less — we're -0.30% vs the S&P's -0.53%, so ahead by ~+0.23% today (our alpha). Eaton slipped into a small loss and is now resting right on its trailing stop; if it dips more it sells itself automatically — the safety net working, not a problem. Book still ~+$4,550 unrealized (GE +14.1%, Lilly +11.9%). ~34% cash. Close routine owns today's scorecard and the weekly review (holiday Friday).
